node.statement: STAGE 2 (Quantum): A block encoding of the Hamiltonian H = C^{1/2}(L+A)^{-1}C^{1/2} can be constructed with block-encoding factor O(log^2(1/h)) and gate complexity O(z poly(log 1/h, log 1/delta)), where z is the number of material regions. This requires subroutines: (a) sparse matrix block encodings of L,A,C via row/column/entry oracles, (b) block encoding of BPX preconditioner F via interpolation operator block encodings, (c) matrix inversion via QSVT, (d) square root via QSVT, (e) linear combination of block encodings. [Paper Sections 6-7, Theorem 13]
